Edward Ka-Spel.title: 10 To The Power Of 9 Chapters 3 & 4
Edward Ka-Spel.about:
I WOULD RATHER BE A DISEMBODIED VOICE THAN A VOICELESS BODY.
FOR A START MY CARBON FOOTPRINT WOULD BE MUCH MORE ACCEPTABLE WITH NO PHYSICAL ATTRIBUTES TO SPEAK OF.
ONE NEEDS TO CONSIDER THE SUBJECT OF VALUABLE RESOURCES  CONSUMED BY EACH OF THESE OPTIONS.
A BODY TAKES UP SPACE, REQUIRES SUSTENANCE AND ULTIMATELY DECAYS, USUALLY IN UNPLEASANT CIRCUMSTANCES.
A VOICE MERELY FADES …INFINITELY. BUT EVEN AS IT FADES, IT COMMUNICATES…...INFINITELY.
I AM THAT VOICE.
YOU MAY NOT LIKE THE THINGS I HAVE TO SAY, BUT YOU WILL NOT SILENCE ME,
IF YOU ARE TECHNICALLY INCLINED , YOU MAY DISTORT, SCRAMBLE AND MASK ME IN A VARIETY OF WAYS.
BUT I WILL SLIP THROUGH THESE PRIMITIVE TRAPS AND SHOUT IF IT’S NEEDED, FROM A PLACE YOU WILL FIND MUCH TOO LATE.
YOU SEE I RELY UPON CHANCE.
IT IS INEVITABLE THAT SOMEWHERE DOWN THERE IN THE MATERIAL WORLD, SOMEONE WANTS TO HEAR ME. AND THEY WILL.
AT THE TURN OF A DIAL. WITH THE SWEEP OF A HAND, THEY TOO WILL BECOME THE ENEMIES OF THOSE WHO WISH TO SILENCE ME.
OF COURSE MY MESSAGE CAN BE INTERPRETED AS A WAVEFORM ACROSS A SCREEN…BUT THAT MERELY MAGNIFIES WHAT I INTEND TO SAY.
PERHAPS THE SILENCERS WILL TRY TO EDIT MY WORDS. PERHAPS THEY WILL TRY TO HUMILIATE ME AND RENDER ME HARMLESS.
SUCH PRACTISES ARE DOOMED.
FOR I AM NOT ALONE.
IN FACT, I’M EVERYWHERE …... .
I’M EVERYWHERE FOR ALL WHO LISTEN.
ALL WHO LISTEN …
LIKE YOU, WHO LISTENS NOW….

Edward Ka-Spel.title: An Unlikely Event (Remaster 2024)
Edward Ka-Spel.about:
It all began so informally. EK had fixed a few low key solo shows around Europe while old friend Nicoletta Stephanz just happened to be on the same continent and had brought her theramin and box of delights with her.
A collaboration seemed like a first class idea although rehearsals were out of the question.
Nicoletta's presence added that extra element of chance - but hell, what a glorious element.
At one point it seemed like she was playing the air itself with a magic wand. EK and Silverman just stared …open-mouthed…Marvellous! Then there was her voice part …..a massive choir of her voice through a myriad of effects. Swathes of improvisation occurred.
In fact the concert represented here (at Stuttgart) for this cdr release was one of the first…..this trio went on to tour the US finishing with a bizarre climax in Seattle where the whole audience joined in with the band in a song about a happy doughnut.
There were other faces onstage too during the U.S leg of the tour in 2003 .Ninah Pixie played a circuit bent flower with the trio in San Francisco while EK's all-time hero Daevid Allen joined the band for the encore section of the same show.
Truly unlikely events and also unforgettable…
A big thank you to Nicoletta , Lisa and Ray.
Edward Ka-Spel.credits:
released January 6, 2024
EK- voice / keyboards; The Silverman - keyboards; Nicoletta Sptephanz- theramin, devices, voice. Raymond Steeg - Sound wizardry.

Edward Ka-Spel.title: Caste O' Graye Skreeëns (2023 Remaster)
Edward Ka-Spel.about:
‘Caste o’ Graye Skreeens’ was the first album in a trilogy loosely alluding to seafaring adventures.
As a child I was a little obsessed by the legend of “The Flying Dutchman” and it was definitely
at the back of my mind as this album from the the start of the millennium was painstakingly constructed.
The main piece ‘Our Captain’s Eyes’ pays tribute to Procol Harum’s ‘A Salty Dog’ , a song that resonated deeply
when I was young and makes me cry to this day.
Sadly “Caste” was seemingly doomed from the beginning. Perhaps the 53 minutes duration of the piece frightened people off
but it was also one of the last releases by World Serpent before the company folded. Consequently it probably never reached
a single shop.
Nevertheless a couple of boxes of the original cd are in stock at Chez Dots.
The 2023 remaster splits “Our Captain’s Eyes” into five parts asI have to concede that my original concept was perhaps too
extreme. The beautiful cover is an original painting by Monadiki.

Edward Ka-Spel.title: Dance China Doll (2020 Remaster)
Edward Ka-Spel.about: 1984. A perfect number for a personal crisis. It's the year when I felt the ideas I had for The Dots were being tossed aside but, upon reflection, I was probably just being a bit of a prima donna. Even so, the situation led to this first collection of songs under my name. Recorded in 3 days , mixed in less than that, with wonderful vocal contributions from Lily Ak (who still sings like an angel). 1984. When the end of the year came, I moved to The Netherlands.
Edward Ka-Spel.credits:
released October 29, 2020
Thank you to Lily Ak for the lovely voice, Pat Birmingham who mixed, Mick Marshall who lent me his DX9 and Sally who provided the doll seen on the cover.

Edward Ka-Spel.title: Eyes China Doll (Original Master 1985)
Edward Ka-Spel.about: This is a release following the transfer of the recently unearthed analogue master tapes of this record from 1985. Digital enhancement has been minimal and those close to this record will hear a marked difference with the cd master. "Eyes" is very much a collaborative album, made together with composer and sound wizard Hero Wouters whose contribution was utterly priceless. A big thank you has to go to Marylou who was responsible for introducing me to Hero, to Freek Kinkelaar who produced the tape and to Ray Steeg who hunted down the machine and the tools to make the dusty masters come alive again.

Edward Ka-Spel.title: Khataclimici China Doll (2021 Remaster)
Edward Ka-Spel.about:
1988
A small untidy room in an Amsterdam squat. An 8-track tape recorder sits at the centre of the floor, the mixing desk was next to it. Wires everywhere. No chairs…Vocals were recorded in between trams. Nothing against trams, they are ecologically sound and sonically fascinating, until the 100th rattling clang of the day and the inevitable pause.
Despite all that Barry Gray (Stret Majest Alarme) turned the place into a palace with his stately guitar, and the desire to experiment was intense.
Even so, the best part was the mixing. A REAL studio in Aachen. Christoph Heemann and Achim Flamm from HNAS firing ideas to turn an already psychedelic record into a full-blown peyote ritual in the depths of the jungle.
Khataclimici China Doll - a damn fine way to end the 80s and a conclusion to the “Aazhyd” trilogy (which is in fact a pair).
Here is the 2021 Remaster - hopefully the ultimate version.
Edward Ka-Spel.credits:
released July 13, 2021
EK- voice, keyboards, devices; The Silverman - Clangs; Stret Majest Alarme - Guitars, drum programming.
Recorded at The Dolls' House, Amsterdam, Bartlos Studio, Aachen
Production: EK & Christoph Heemann
Cover Art: Christoph Heemann
Engineer: Ramon Creutzer

Edward Ka-Spel.title: Live at Cafe Oto 18 May 2024
Edward Ka-Spel.about:
Maybe the best festivals are the small ones.
Cafe Oto in North East London can only hold 180 people but it was packed to the rafters for the Fourth Dimension Festival.Also on the bill was the truly legendary Alternative TV, Splintered and Steven Wilson's Bass Communion collaborating with Modelbau (Frans de Waard). The atmosphere was glorious, the music varied and always uplifting, and ultimately it was a Spring night not to be forgotten. Fellow Pink Dot Randall Frazier joined me on stage while Raymond Steeg was at the mixing desk. Thanks to Richo, who organised; Alena, who drove; Ruth, who filmed the event and Elisa, who helped. A small CDr edition of this album is planned in the near future.
Edward Ka-Spel.credits:
released June 3, 2024
EK- vox, occasional keyboards; Randall Frazier- keyboards, devices; Raymond Steeg- sound wizardry, mastering.

Edward Ka-Spel.title: Lyvv China Doll Redux (2024 Master)
Edward Ka-Spel.about:
"Lyvv China Doll" started life as an elaborate but very elusive cassette release from The Dots' TEKA label- it was half live material and half comprised of bits and pieces that needed a home.
Later it became a cd for the Staalplaat label and the live side was dropped while more bits and pieces came into view.This remaster brings the two releases together.
The first live piece in the track listing is actually "The Horn Section" while a little later the first ever performance of "So Gallantly Screaming." can be heard as a mere idea rather than a finished song. The year was 1985 and the venue was in the village of Delden in the far East of The Netherlands.
Never heard of Delden? Guess what, they hadn't heard of us either (EK, Ignit, Ed van Kaye)..You could say the reaction was minimal when the screaming started; it even became mildly hostile when that horn section (actually miniature plastic saxophones from the gas station) were introduced. I'd like to think they still talk about it in Delden but they probably forgot decades ago.
Other live tracks were from an especially heavy solo show in Vienna (1988) and Strasbourg (1986) where The Silverman and Stret Majest Alarme completed a particularly incendiary line-up.
Other notable songs from Lyvv" include "Tower 8" and "When Old Gods Die" (with Patrick Q.Paganini) which were the famous "missing " bonus songs from the original "Chyekk China Doll" cd. After this mysterious omission, the master tapes were delivered to a guy in Sweden for an intended 7" release. Sadly tapes and guy disappeared. Thankfully EK had rough cassette copies in his drawer .
Finally I must thank Hero Wouters for his amazing work on "Dada" and "Beautiful Plastic" (he wrote the music for the latter piece). Always a joy to work with Hero....Thanks too to Bianca Wouters for her voice ...This is the enhanced 2024 master. EK

Edward Ka-Spel.title: One Last Pose Before the Ruin (Expanded 2024)
Edward Ka-Spel.about:
IN THE PRESENCE OF THE LENS I WILL SUSPEND MY PLAUSIBILITY AND POUT FOR ALL ETERNITY MISSHAPEN, SAD AND
AWKWARD.YOU SEE, I CANNOT GET MY MOUTH QUITE RIGHT, ITʼS LIKE I'M BEING TORTURED.. AND MY MELANCHOLY SOUL HAS JUST BEEN KIDNAPPED THEN DEPORTED.......AHHH
BUT THEYʼLL TAKE AWAY MY BLEMISHES AND GIVE MY HAIR SOME STYLE TWO BRIGHT BLUE EYES TO LOOK THROUGH AND A MILLION DOLLAR SMILE
A BODY THATʼS BEEN WORKING OUT, THEYʼLL PAINT OVER ALL THOSE TEARS AND MY PRECIOUS PLACE UPON THIS EARTH WILL DISAPPEAR........
LAST, THEY ‘LL STRIP ME OF MY NAME, THEN THEY'LL BRAND ME WITH A CODE THATʼS COMPOSED OF ONES AND ZEROES THAT STRETCH ACROSS THE ROAD. AND I ENVY MY DEAR FOREFATHERS IN SEPIA AND CHROME; THE STEADY FADE HAS SERVED YOU WELL ....ALTHOUGH ITʼS SLOW.

Edward Ka-Spel.title: The Perfect Patch (Berlin - Amsterdam in the 1980s)
Edward Ka-Spel.about:
This is a release for die-hards only. It’s the sound of Edward Ka-Spel lost in the Zone with synthesisers and effects units back in 1981, later developed a little ,but never heard by the wider World in it’s entirety until a few years ago. There have been snatches revealed, on “Kleine Krieg” in particular but the full-on Kosmisch Attack was perhaps considered too indulgent for public consumption during the previous 36 years.
Even so, the session had a tantalising history. It was largely created in Conrad Schnitzler’s mighty music room in Berlin. EK stayed with him in 1981, ostensibly to make an interview for a fanzine (which sadly never appeared ), and the great pioneer kindly gave him shelter for almost a week.
One night Conrad had a dinner appointment and invited The Pink Dots' singer to “do whatever he wanted “ on the synths and echo chambers scattered around the magical space.
It was the equivalent of 15 Christmases arriving at once. He indulged himself and recorded the results for posterity. Bless you Conrad…we all miss you!
November 15 2018…After several requests, the "B" side of the cassette edition was added to the original release. "An Amsterdam Space Jam" was created a little later in the 80s and is culled from several hours of improvisation on the Yamaha CS30 synthesiser which EK still owns and loves.
Alas, the Cassette edition is long gone, and a very limited CD-R edition is in the works as this missive is being typed.

Edward Ka-Spel.title: The Textures of Illumina (2024 Remaster)
Edward Ka-Spel.about:
An album that was first released on vinyl as a 10", then greatly expanded with bonus tracks when it later emerged as a double cd. This is the ultimate version (including those bonus tracks) after an intense sonic overhaul. To complete the rebirth, it has been given a new cover .
Tracks 1 - 3 represent the original 10” album. Tracks 4 - 6 are bonus cuts from compilation albums in the late 90s. Tracks 7 - 12 was a suite of songs/ sketches old and new that formed the bonus disc of the 2 x cd release on Soleilmoon Recordings.
